rulings.Case Title: ELAINE PERCY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, March 5, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Pacific Bell Telephone Company¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is granted as to issues 1 and 3 (negligence) and denied as to issues 2 and 4 (strict liability). As to the negligence causes of action, defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiffs did not produce admissible evidence that defendant knew or reasonably should have known that the cleaning up, sweeping and/or disturbance of asbestos-containing material could create a safety hazard of secondary exposure for a member of a household of a non-employee. Even if decedent¿s injury was foreseeable, pursuant to Rowland v. Christian (1968) 69 Cal.2d 108 and Campbell v. Ford Motor Company (2012) 206 Cal.App.4th15, public policy dictates against a creation of a duty in the present matter. As to the strict liability causes of action, defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to present evidence negating an essential element of plaintiffs¿ strict liability causes of action. Defendant also failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence to support their strict liability causes of action. By failing to demonstrate that it propounded contention discovery designed to elicit all facts and evidence plaintiffs have to support their claims for strict liability, and by failing to demonstrate that plaintiffs submitted factually devoid responses thereto, defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE COURIS VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Dismiss Action; Supporting Declaration Of David L. Barch; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities And Proposed Order
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, March 5, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendant Imo Industries Inc.¿s motion to dismiss entire action pursuant to C.C.P. 583.310 and 583.360 is granted. No opposition filed and more than five years has passed since plaintiff commenced this action. Hearing required unless counsel for defendant notifies Mr. Couris of the tentative ruling and he states that he does not wish to contest. The hearing will be at 9:45a.m. Imo Industries must bring a proposed order to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. = (503/TLJ)
